nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token. You can use this token in a subsequent request to retrieve the next set of applications.

applications.description: An array of summary information on the configuration of one or more Amazon Q Business applications.

displayName.description: The name of the Amazon Q Business application.

applicationId.description: The identifier for the Amazon Q Business application.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business application was created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business application was last updated.

status.description: The status of the Amazon Q Business application. The application is ready to use when the status is ACTIVE.

identityType.description: The authentication type being used by a Amazon Q Business application.

quickSightConfiguration.description: The Amazon QuickSight configuration for an Amazon Q Business application that uses QuickSight as the identity provider.

clientNamespace.description: The Amazon QuickSight namespace that is used as the identity provider. For more information about QuickSight namespaces, see Namespace operations.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of plugin metadata.

items.description: An array of information on plugin metadata.

description.description: The description assigned by Amazon Q Business to a plugin. You can't modify this value.

statement.description: The JSON representation of the added permission statement.

failedDocuments.description: A list of documents that couldn't be removed from the Amazon Q Business index. Each entry contains an error message that indicates why the document couldn't be removed from the index.

id.description: The identifier of the document that couldn't be removed from the Amazon Q Business index.

error.description: An explanation for why the document couldn't be removed from the index.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

dataSourceId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business data source connector that contains the failed document.

failedDocuments.description: A list of documents that were not added to the Amazon Q Business index because the document failed a validation check. Each document contains an error message that indicates why the document couldn't be added to the index.

id.description: The identifier of the document that couldn't be removed from the Amazon Q Business index.

error.description: An explanation for why the document couldn't be removed from the index.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

dataSourceId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business data source connector that contains the failed document.

subscriptionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Q Business subscription being cancelled.

currentSubscription.description: The type of your current Amazon Q Business subscription.

nextSubscription.description: The type of the Amazon Q Business subscription for the next month.

type.description: The type of an Amazon Q Business subscription.

textEvent.description: Information about the payload of the ChatOutputStream event containing the AI-generated message output.

systemMessageType.description:
The type of AI-generated message in a TextOutputEvent. Amazon Q Business currently supports two types of messages:

  • RESPONSE - The Amazon Q Business system response.

  • GROUNDED_RESPONSE - The corrected, hallucination-reduced, response returned by Amazon Q Business. Available only if hallucination reduction is supported and configured for the application and detected in the end user chat query by Amazon Q Business.

conversationId.description: The identifier of the conversation with which the text output event is associated.

userMessageId.description: The identifier of an end user message in a TextOutputEvent.

systemMessageId.description: The identifier of an AI-generated message in a TextOutputEvent.

metadataEvent.description: A metadata event for a AI-generated text output message in a Amazon Q Business conversation.

conversationId.description: The identifier of the conversation with which the generated metadata is associated.

userMessageId.description: The identifier of an Amazon Q Business end user text input message within the conversation.

systemMessageId.description: The identifier of an Amazon Q Business AI generated message within the conversation.

sourceAttributions.description: The source documents used to generate the conversation response.

finalTextMessage.description: The final text output message generated by the system.

actionReviewEvent.description: A request from Amazon Q Business to the end user for information Amazon Q Business needs to successfully complete a requested plugin action.

conversationId.description: The identifier of the conversation with which the action review event is associated.

userMessageId.description: The identifier of the conversation with which the plugin action is associated.

systemMessageId.description: The identifier of an Amazon Q Business AI generated associated with the action review event.

pluginId.description: The identifier of the plugin associated with the action review event.

payload.description: Field values that an end user needs to provide to Amazon Q Business for Amazon Q Business to perform the requested plugin action.

payloadFieldNameSeparator.description: A string used to retain information about the hierarchical contexts within an action review event payload.

failedAttachmentEvent.description: A failed file upload event during a web experience chat.

conversationId.description: The identifier of the conversation associated with the failed file upload.

userMessageId.description: The identifier of the end user chat message associated with the file upload.

systemMessageId.description: The identifier of the AI-generated message associated with the file upload.

attachment.description: The details of a file uploaded during chat.

name.description: The name of a file uploaded during chat.

status.description: The status of a file uploaded during chat.

error.description: An error associated with a file uploaded during chat.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

attachmentId.description: The unique identifier of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

conversationId.description: The unique identifier of the Amazon Q Business conversation.

authChallengeRequestEvent.description: An authentication verification event activated by an end user request to use a custom plugin.

authorizationUrl.description: The URL sent by Amazon Q Business to a third party authentication server in response to an authentication verification event activated by an end user request to use a custom plugin.

conversationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business conversation.

systemMessageId.description: The identifier of an Amazon Q Business AI generated message within the conversation.

userMessageId.description: The identifier of an Amazon Q Business end user text input message within the conversation.

actionReview.description: A request from Amazon Q Business to the end user for information Amazon Q Business needs to successfully complete a requested plugin action.

authChallengeRequest.description: An authentication verification event activated by an end user request to use a custom plugin.

sourceAttributions.description: The source documents used to generate the conversation response.

failedAttachments.description: A list of files which failed to upload during chat.

title.description: The title of the document which is the source for the Amazon Q Business generated response.

snippet.description: The content extract from the document on which the generated response is based.

url.description: The URL of the document which is the source for the Amazon Q Business generated response.

citationNumber.description: The number attached to a citation in an Amazon Q Business generated response.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business application was last updated.

textMessageSegments.description: A text extract from a source document that is used for source attribution.

documentId.description: The unique identifier of the source document used in the citation, obtained from the Amazon Q Business index during chat response generation. This ID is used as input to the GetDocumentContent API to retrieve the actual document content for user verification.

indexId.description: The identifier of the index containing the source document's metadata and access control information. This links the citation back to the specific Amazon Q Business index where the document's searchable content and permissions are stored.

datasourceId.description: The identifier of the data source from which the document was ingested. This field is not present if the document is ingested by directly calling the BatchPutDocument API (similar to checkDocumentAccess). If the document is from a file-upload data source, the datasource will be "uploaded-docs-file-stat-datasourceid".

userGroups.description: An array of groups the user is part of for the specified data source. Each group has a name and type.

userAliases.description: An array of aliases associated with the user. This includes both global and local aliases, each with a name and type.

hasAccess.description: A boolean value indicating whether the specified user has access to the document, either direct access or transitive access via groups and aliases attached to the document.

documentAcl.description: The Access Control List (ACL) associated with the document. Includes allowlist and denylist conditions that determine user access.

name.description: The name of the group associated with the user. This is used to identify the group in access control decisions.

type.description: The type of the associated group. This indicates the scope of the group's applicability.

anonymousUrl.description:
The unique URL for accessing the web experience.

This URL can only be used once and must be used within 5 minutes after it's generated.

applicationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business application.

applicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Q Business application.

chatResponseConfigurationId.description: The unique identifier assigned to a newly created chat response configuration, used for subsequent operations on this resource.

chatResponseConfigurationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the newly created chat response configuration, which uniquely identifies the resource across all Amazon Web Services services.

dataAccessorId.description: The unique identifier of the created data accessor.

idcApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center application created for this data accessor.

dataAccessorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the created data accessor.

dataSourceId.description: The identifier of the data source connector.

dataSourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a data source in an Amazon Q Business application.

indexId.description: The identifier for the Amazon Q Business index.

indexArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an Amazon Q Business index.

pluginArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a plugin.

buildStatus.description: The current status of a plugin. A plugin is modified asynchronously.

retrieverId.description: The identifier of the retriever you are using.

retrieverArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an IAM role associated with a retriever.

subscriptionId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business subscription created.

subscriptionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Q Business subscription created.

currentSubscription.description: The type of your current Amazon Q Business subscription.

nextSubscription.description: The type of the Amazon Q Business subscription for the next month.

type.description: The type of an Amazon Q Business subscription.

webExperienceId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business web experience.

webExperienceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an Amazon Q Business web experience.

displayName.description: The name of the Amazon Q Business application.

applicationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business application.

applicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Q Business application.

identityType.description: The authentication type being used by a Amazon Q Business application.

iamIdentityProviderArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an identity provider being used by an Amazon Q Business application.

identityCenterApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS IAM Identity Center instance attached to your Amazon Q Business application.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM with permissions to access your CloudWatch logs and metrics.

status.description: The status of the Amazon Q Business application.

description.description: A description for the Amazon Q Business application.

encryptionConfiguration.description: The identifier of the Amazon Web Services KMS key that is used to encrypt your data. Amazon Q Business doesn't support asymmetric keys.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business application was last updated.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business application was last updated.

error.description: If the Status field is set to ERROR, the ErrorMessage field contains a description of the error that caused the synchronization to fail.

attachmentsConfiguration.description: Settings for whether end users can upload files directly during chat.

qAppsConfiguration.description: Settings for whether end users can create and use Amazon Q Apps in the web experience.

personalizationConfiguration.description: Configuration information about chat response personalization. For more information, see Personalizing chat responses.

autoSubscriptionConfiguration.description: Settings for auto-subscription behavior for this application. This is only applicable to SAML and OIDC applications.

clientIdsForOIDC.description: The OIDC client ID for a Amazon Q Business application.

quickSightConfiguration.description: The Amazon QuickSight authentication configuration for the Amazon Q Business application.

responseScope.description: The response scope configured for a Amazon Q Business application. This determines whether your application uses its retrieval augmented generation (RAG) system to generate answers only from your enterprise data, or also uses the large language models (LLM) knowledge to respons to end user questions in chat.

orchestrationConfiguration.description:
The chat response orchestration settings for your application.

Chat orchestration is optimized to work for English language content. For more details on language support in Amazon Q Business, see Supported languages.

blockedPhrases.description: The phrases blocked from chat by your chat control configuration.

topicConfigurations.description: The topic specific controls configured for a Amazon Q Business application.

nextToken.description: If the maxResults response was incomplete because there is more data to retrieve, Amazon Q Business returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of Amazon Q Business chat controls configured.

hallucinationReductionConfiguration.description: The hallucination reduction settings for your application.

name.description: A name for your topic control configuration.

description.description: A description for your topic control configuration. Use this to outline how the large language model (LLM) should use this topic control configuration.

exampleChatMessages.description: A list of example phrases that you expect the end user to use in relation to the topic.

chatResponseConfigurationId.description: The unique identifier of the retrieved chat response configuration.

chatResponseConfigurationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the retrieved chat response configuration, which uniquely identifies the resource across all Amazon Web Services services.

displayName.description: The human-readable name of the retrieved chat response configuration, making it easier to identify among multiple configurations.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the chat response configuration was initially created.

inUseConfiguration.description: The currently active configuration settings that are being used to generate responses in the Amazon Q Business application.

lastUpdateConfiguration.description: Information about the most recent update to the configuration, including timestamp and modification details.

responseConfigurations.description: A collection of specific response configuration settings that collectively define how responses are generated, formatted, and presented to users in chat interactions.

responseConfigurationSummary.description: A summary of the response configuration details, providing a concise overview of the key parameters and settings that define the response generation behavior.

status.description: The current status of the chat response configuration, indicating whether it is active, pending, or in another state that affects its availability for use.

error.description: Provides information about a Amazon Q Business request error.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the detailed chat response configuration was last modified, helping administrators track changes and maintain version awareness.

displayName.description: The friendly name of the data accessor.

dataAccessorId.description: The unique identifier of the data accessor.

dataAccessorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the data accessor.

applicationId.description: The unique identifier of the Amazon Q Business application associated with this data accessor.

idcApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center application associated with this data accessor.

principal.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role for the ISV associated with this data accessor.

actionConfigurations.description: The list of action configurations specifying the allowed actions and any associated filters.

authenticationDetail.description: The authentication configuration details for the data accessor. This specifies how the ISV authenticates when accessing data through this data accessor.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the data accessor was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the data accessor was last updated.

action.description: The Amazon Q Business action that is allowed.

filterConfiguration.description: The filter configuration for the action, if any.

documentAttributeFilter.description: Enables filtering of responses based on document attributes or metadata fields.

andAllFilters.description: Performs a logical AND operation on all supplied filters.

orAllFilters.description: Performs a logical OR operation on all supplied filters.

notFilter.description: Performs a logical NOT operation on all supplied filters.

andAllFilters.description: Performs a logical AND operation on all supplied filters.

orAllFilters.description: Performs a logical OR operation on all supplied filters.

notFilter.description: Performs a logical NOT operation on all supplied filters.

equalsTo.description: Performs an equals operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue, longValue, stringListValue and stringValue.

containsAll.description: Returns true when a document contains all the specified document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: stringListValue.

containsAny.description: Returns true when a document contains any of the specified document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: stringListValue.

greaterThan.description: Performs a greater than operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue and longValue.

greaterThanOrEquals.description: Performs a greater or equals than operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue and longValue.

lessThan.description: Performs a less than operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue and longValue.

lessThanOrEquals.description: Performs a less than or equals operation on two document attributes or metadata fields.Supported for the following document attribute value type: dateValue and longValue.

equalsTo.description: Performs an equals operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue, longValue, stringListValue and stringValue.

containsAll.description: Returns true when a document contains all the specified document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: stringListValue.

containsAny.description: Returns true when a document contains any of the specified document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: stringListValue.

greaterThan.description: Performs a greater than operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue and longValue.

greaterThanOrEquals.description: Performs a greater or equals than operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue and longValue.

lessThan.description: Performs a less than operation on two document attributes or metadata fields. Supported for the following document attribute value types: dateValue and longValue.

lessThanOrEquals.description: Performs a less than or equals operation on two document attributes or metadata fields.Supported for the following document attribute value type: dateValue and longValue.

applicationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business application.

indexId.description: The identifier of the index linked to the data source connector.

dataSourceId.description: The identifier of the data source connector.

dataSourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the data source.

displayName.description: The name for the data source connector.

type.description: The type of the data source connector. For example, S3.

configuration.description: The details of how the data source connector is configured.

vpcConfiguration.description: Configuration information for an Amazon VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) to connect to your data source.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the data source connector was created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the data source connector was last updated.

description.description: The description for the data source connector.

status.description: The current status of the data source connector. When the Status field value is FAILED, the ErrorMessage field contains a description of the error that caused the data source connector to fail.

syncSchedule.description: The schedule for Amazon Q Business to update the index.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role with permission to access the data source and required resources.

error.description: When the Status field value is FAILED, the ErrorMessage field contains a description of the error that caused the data source connector to fail.

documentEnrichmentConfiguration.description:
Provides the configuration information for altering document metadata and content during the document ingestion process.

For more information, see Custom document enrichment.

mediaExtractionConfiguration.description: The configuration for extracting information from media in documents for the data source.

presignedUrl.description: A pre-signed URL that provides temporary access to download the document content directly from Amazon Q Business. The URL expires after 5 minutes for security purposes. This URL is generated only after successful ACL validation.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the document content. When outputFormat is RAW, this corresponds to the original document's MIME type (e.g., application/pdf, text/plain, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document). When outputFormat is EXTRACTED, the MIME type is always application/json.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business application was last updated.

errorDetail.description: The details of an error associated a group status.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

applicationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business application associated with the index.

indexId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business index.

displayName.description: The name of the Amazon Q Business index.

indexArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Q Business index.

status.description: The current status of the index. When the value is ACTIVE, the index is ready for use. If the Status field value is FAILED, the ErrorMessage field contains a message that explains why.

type.description: The type of index attached to your Amazon Q Business application.

description.description: The description for the Amazon Q Business index.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business index was created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business index was last updated.

capacityConfiguration.description: The storage capacity units chosen for your Amazon Q Business index.

documentAttributeConfigurations.description: Configuration information for document attributes or metadata. Document metadata are fields associated with your documents. For example, the company department name associated with each document. For more information, see Understanding document attributes.

error.description: When the Status field value is FAILED, the ErrorMessage field contains a message that explains why.

indexStatistics.description: Provides information about the number of documents indexed.

search.description: Information about whether the document attribute can be used by an end user to search for information on their web experience.

mediaBytes.description: The base64-encoded bytes of the media object.

mediaMimeType.description: The MIME type of the media object (image/png).

applicationId.description: The identifier of the application which contains the plugin.

serverUrl.description: The source URL used for plugin configuration.

authConfiguration.description: Authentication configuration information for an Amazon Q Business plugin.

customPluginConfiguration.description: Configuration information required to create a custom plugin.

buildStatus.description: The current status of a plugin. A plugin is modified asynchronously.

pluginArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role with permission to access resources needed to create the plugin.

createdAt.description: The timestamp for when the plugin was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp for when the plugin was last updated.

basicAuthConfiguration.description: Information about the basic authentication credentials used to configure a plugin.

secretArn.description: The ARN of the Secrets Manager secret that stores the basic authentication credentials used for plugin configuration..

roleArn.description: The ARN of an IAM role used by Amazon Q Business to access the basic authentication credentials stored in a Secrets Manager secret.

oAuth2ClientCredentialConfiguration.description: Information about the OAuth 2.0 authentication credential/token used to configure a plugin.

secretArn.description: The ARN of the Secrets Manager secret that stores the OAuth 2.0 credentials/token used for plugin configuration.

roleArn.description: The ARN of an IAM role used by Amazon Q Business to access the OAuth 2.0 authentication credentials stored in a Secrets Manager secret.

authorizationUrl.description: The redirect URL required by the OAuth 2.0 protocol for Amazon Q Business to authenticate a plugin user through a third party authentication server.

tokenUrl.description: The URL required by the OAuth 2.0 protocol to exchange an end user authorization code for an access token.

noAuthConfiguration.description: Information about invoking a custom plugin without any authentication.

idcAuthConfiguration.description: Information about the IAM Identity Center Application used to configure authentication for a plugin.

idcApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center Application used to configure authentication.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role with permissions to perform actions on Amazon Web Services services on your behalf.

policy.description: The JSON representation of the permission policy.

applicationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business application using the retriever.

retrieverArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role associated with the retriever.

configuration.description: Provides information on how the retriever used for your Amazon Q Business application is configured.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role with the permission to access the retriever and required resources.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the retriever was created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the retriever was last updated.

nativeIndexConfiguration.description: Provides information on how a Amazon Q Business index used as a retriever for your Amazon Q Business application is configured.

indexId.description: The identifier for the Amazon Q Business index.

version.description:
A read-only field that specifies the version of the NativeIndexConfiguration.

Amazon Q Business introduces enhanced document retrieval capabilities in version 2 of NativeIndexConfiguration, focusing on streamlined metadata boosting that prioritizes recency and source relevance to deliver more accurate responses to your queries. Version 2 has the following differences from version 1:

  • Version 2 supports a single Date field (created_at OR last_updated_at) for recency boosting

  • Version 2 supports a single String field with an ordered list of up to 5 values

  • Version 2 introduces number-based boost levels (ONE, TWO) alongside the text-based levels

  • Version 2 allows specifying prioritization between Date and String fields

  • Version 2 maintains backward compatibility with existing configurations

boostingOverride.description: Overrides the default boosts applied by Amazon Q Business to supported document attribute data types.

kendraIndexConfiguration.description: Provides information on how the Amazon Kendra index used as a retriever for your Amazon Q Business application is configured.

indexId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Kendra index.

userAliases.description: A list of user aliases attached to a user.

indexId.description: The identifier of the index that the user aliases are associated with.

dataSourceId.description: The identifier of the data source that the user aliases are associated with.

userId.description: The identifier of the user id associated with the user aliases.

applicationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business application linked to the web experience.

webExperienceId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business web experience.

webExperienceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role with the permission to access the Amazon Q Business web experience and required resources.

defaultEndpoint.description: The endpoint of your Amazon Q Business web experience.

status.description: The current status of the Amazon Q Business web experience. When the Status field value is FAILED, the ErrorMessage field contains a description of the error that caused the data source connector to fail.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business web experience was last created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business web experience was last updated.

title.description: The title for your Amazon Q Business web experience.

subtitle.description: The subtitle for your Amazon Q Business web experience.

welcomeMessage.description: The customized welcome message for end users of an Amazon Q Business web experience.

samplePromptsControlMode.description: Determines whether sample prompts are enabled in the web experience for an end user.

origins.description: Gets the website domain origins that are allowed to embed the Amazon Q Business web experience. The domain origin refers to the base URL for accessing a website including the protocol (http/https), the domain name, and the port number (if specified).

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the service role attached to your web experience.

identityProviderConfiguration.description: Information about the identity provider (IdP) used to authenticate end users of an Amazon Q Business web experience.

authenticationConfiguration.description: The authentication configuration information for your Amazon Q Business web experience.

error.description: When the Status field value is FAILED, the ErrorMessage field contains a description of the error that caused the data source connector to fail.

browserExtensionConfiguration.description: The browser extension configuration for an Amazon Q Business web experience.

customizationConfiguration.description: Gets the custom logo, favicon, font, and color used in the Amazon Q web experience.

attachments.description: An array of information on one or more attachments.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of attachments.

attachmentId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

conversationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business conversation the attachment is associated with.

name.description: Filename of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

copyFrom.description: A CopyFromSource containing a reference to the original source of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

conversation.description: A reference to an attachment in an existing conversation.

conversationId.description: The unique identifier of the Amazon Q Business conversation.

attachmentId.description: The unique identifier of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

fileType.description: Filetype of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

fileSize.description: Size in bytes of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

md5chksum.description: MD5 checksum of the Amazon Q Business attachment contents.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business attachment was created.

status.description: AttachmentStatus of the Amazon Q Business attachment.

error.description: ErrorDetail providing information about a Amazon Q Business attachment error.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

chatResponseConfigurations.description: A list of chat response configuration summaries, each containing key information about an available configuration in the specified application.

nextToken.description: A pagination token that can be used in a subsequent request to retrieve additional chat response configurations if the results were truncated due to the maxResults parameter.

chatResponseConfigurationId.description: A unique identifier for your chat response configuration settings, used to reference and manage the configuration within the Amazon Q Business service.

chatResponseConfigurationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the chat response configuration, which uniquely identifies the resource across all Amazon Web Services services and accounts.

displayName.description: A human-readable name for the chat response configuration, making it easier to identify and manage multiple configurations within an organization.

responseConfigurationSummary.description: A summary of the response configuration settings, providing a concise overview of the key parameters that define how responses are generated and formatted.

status.description: The current status of the chat response configuration, indicating whether it is active, pending, or in another state that affects its availability for use in chat interactions.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the chat response configuration was initially created, useful for tracking the lifecycle of configuration resources.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the chat response configuration was last modified, helping administrators track changes and maintain version awareness.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of messages.

conversations.description: An array of summary information on the configuration of one or more Amazon Q Business web experiences.

conversationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business conversation.

nextToken.description: The token to use to retrieve the next set of results, if there are any.

displayName.description: The friendly name of the data accessor.

dataAccessorId.description: The unique identifier of the data accessor.

dataAccessorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the data accessor.

idcApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the associated IAM Identity Center application.

principal.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role for the ISV associated with this data accessor.

authenticationDetail.description: The authentication configuration details for the data accessor. This specifies how the ISV authenticates when accessing data through this data accessor.

authenticationType.description:
The type of authentication to use for the data accessor. This determines how the ISV authenticates when accessing data. You can use one of two authentication types:

  • AWS_IAM_IDC_TTI - Authentication using IAM Identity Center Trusted Token Issuer (TTI). This authentication type allows the ISV to use a trusted token issuer to generate tokens for accessing the data.

  • AWS_IAM_IDC_AUTH_CODE - Authentication using IAM Identity Center authorization code flow. This authentication type uses the standard OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for authentication.

authenticationConfiguration.description: The specific authentication configuration based on the authentication type.

idcTrustedTokenIssuerConfiguration.description: Configuration for IAM Identity Center Trusted Token Issuer (TTI) authentication used when the authentication type is AWS_IAM_IDC_TTI.

idcTrustedTokenIssuerArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center Trusted Token Issuer that will be used for authentication.

externalIds.description: A list of external identifiers associated with this authentication configuration. These are used to correlate the data accessor with external systems.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the data accessor was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the data accessor was last updated.

history.description: A history of synchronization jobs for the data source connector.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token. You can use this token in any subsequent request to retrieve the next set of jobs.

executionId.description: The identifier of a data source synchronization job.

startTime.description: The Unix time stamp when the data source synchronization job started.

endTime.description: The Unix timestamp when the synchronization job completed.

status.description: The status of the synchronization job. When the Status field is set to SUCCEEDED, the synchronization job is done. If the status code is FAILED, the ErrorCode and ErrorMessage fields give you the reason for the failure.

error.description: If the Status field is set to FAILED, the ErrorCode field indicates the reason the synchronization failed.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

dataSourceErrorCode.description: If the reason that the synchronization failed is due to an error with the underlying data source, this field contains a code that identifies the error.

metrics.description: Maps a batch delete document request to a specific data source sync job. This is optional and should only be supplied when documents are deleted by a data source connector.

documentsAdded.description: The current count of documents added from the data source during the data source sync.

documentsModified.description: The current count of documents modified in the data source during the data source sync.

documentsDeleted.description: The current count of documents deleted from the data source during the data source sync.

documentsFailed.description: The current count of documents that failed to sync from the data source during the data source sync.

documentsScanned.description: The current count of documents crawled by the ongoing sync job in the data source.

dataSources.description: An array of summary information for one or more data source connector.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token. You can use this token in a subsequent request to retrieve the next set of data source connectors.

displayName.description: The name of the Amazon Q Business data source.

dataSourceId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business data source.

type.description: The type of the Amazon Q Business data source.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business data source was created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business data source was last updated.

status.description: The status of the Amazon Q Business data source.

nextToken.description: If the maxResults response was incomplete because there is more data to retrieve, Amazon Q Business returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of documents.

error.description: An error message associated with the document.

errorMessage.description: The message explaining the Amazon Q Business request error.

errorCode.description: The code associated with the Amazon Q Business request error.

createdAt.description: The timestamp for when the document was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp for when the document was last updated.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of groups that are mapped to users.

items.description: Summary information for list of groups that are mapped to users.

groupName.description: The name of the group the summary information is for.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of indexes.

indices.description: An array of information on the items in one or more indexes.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the index was created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the index was last updated.

status.description: The current status of the index. When the status is ACTIVE, the index is ready.

messages.description: An array of information on one or more messages.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of messages.

messageId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business web experience message.

body.description: The content of the Amazon Q Business web experience message.

time.description: The timestamp of the first Amazon Q Business web experience message.

type.description: The type of Amazon Q Business message, whether HUMAN or AI generated.

attachments.description: A file directly uploaded into an Amazon Q Business web experience chat.

sourceAttribution.description: The source documents used to generate Amazon Q Business web experience message.

actionReview.description: An output event that Amazon Q Business returns to an user who wants to perform a plugin action during a non-streaming chat conversation. It contains information about the selected action with a list of possible user input fields, some pre-populated by Amazon Q Business.

pluginId.description: The identifier of the plugin associated with the action review.

payload.description: Field values that an end user needs to provide to Amazon Q Business for Amazon Q Business to perform the requested plugin action.

payloadFieldNameSeparator.description: A string used to retain information about the hierarchical contexts within an action review payload.

actionExecution.description: Performs an Amazon Q Business plugin action during a non-streaming chat conversation.

pluginId.description: The identifier of the plugin the action is attached to.

payload.description: A mapping of field names to the field values in input that an end user provides to Amazon Q Business requests to perform their plugin action.

payloadFieldNameSeparator.description: A string used to retain information about the hierarchical contexts within an action execution event payload.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of plugin actions.

items.description: An array of information on one or more plugin actions.

actionIdentifier.description: The identifier of an Amazon Q Business plugin action.

displayName.description: The display name assigned by Amazon Q Business to a plugin action. You can't modify this value.

instructionExample.description:
An Amazon Q Business suggested prompt and end user can use to invoke a plugin action. This value can be modified and sent as input to initiate an action. For example:

  • Create a Jira task

  • Create a chat assistant task to find the root cause of a specific incident

description.description: The description of an Amazon Q Business plugin action.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of plugins.

items.description: An array of information on one or more plugins.

actionIdentifier.description: The identifier of an Amazon Q Business plugin action.

displayName.description: The display name assigned by Amazon Q Business to a plugin action. You can't modify this value.

instructionExample.description:
An Amazon Q Business suggested prompt and end user can use to invoke a plugin action. This value can be modified and sent as input to initiate an action. For example:

  • Create a Jira task

  • Create a chat assistant task to find the root cause of a specific incident

description.description: The description of an Amazon Q Business plugin action.

nextToken.description: If the maxResults response was incomplete because there is more data to retrieve, Amazon Q Business returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of plugins.

serverUrl.description: The plugin server URL used for configuration.

createdAt.description: The timestamp for when the plugin was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp for when the plugin was last updated.

retrievers.description: An array of summary information for one or more retrievers.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of retrievers.

applicationId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business application using the retriever.

retrieverId.description: The identifier of the retriever used by your Amazon Q Business application.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token. You can use this token in a subsequent request to retrieve the next set of subscriptions.

subscriptions.description: An array of summary information on the subscriptions configured for an Amazon Q Business application.

subscriptionId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Q Business subscription to be updated.

subscriptionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Q Business subscription that was updated.

principal.description: The IAM Identity Center UserId or GroupId of a user or group in the IAM Identity Center instance connected to the Amazon Q Business application.

user.description: The identifier of a user in the IAM Identity Center instance connected to the Amazon Q Business application.

group.description: The identifier of a group in the IAM Identity Center instance connected to the Amazon Q Business application.

currentSubscription.description: The type of your current Amazon Q Business subscription.

type.description: The type of an Amazon Q Business subscription.

nextSubscription.description: The type of the Amazon Q Business subscription for the next month.

type.description: The type of an Amazon Q Business subscription.

tags.description: A list of tags associated with the Amazon Q Business application or data source.

key.description: The key for the tag. Keys are not case sensitive and must be unique for the Amazon Q Business application or data source.

value.description: The value associated with the tag. The value may be an empty string but it can't be null.

webExperiences.description: An array of summary information for one or more Amazon Q Business experiences.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Q Business returns this token, which you can use in a later request to list the next set of messages.

webExperienceId.description: The identifier of your Amazon Q Business web experience.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the Amazon Q Business application was last updated.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp when your Amazon Q Business web experience was updated.

defaultEndpoint.description: The endpoint URLs for your Amazon Q Business web experience. The URLs are unique and fully hosted by Amazon Web Services.

status.description: The status of your Amazon Q Business web experience.

relevantContent.description: The list of relevant content items found.

nextToken.description: The token to use to retrieve the next set of results, if there are any.

content.description: The actual content of the relevant item.

documentId.description: The unique identifier of the document containing the relevant content.

documentTitle.description: The title of the document containing the relevant content.

documentUri.description: The URI of the document containing the relevant content.

documentAttributes.description: Additional attributes of the document containing the relevant content.

scoreAttributes.description: Attributes related to the relevance score of the content.

scoreConfidence.description: The confidence level of the relevance score.

executionId.description: The identifier for a particular synchronization job.

subscriptionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Q Business subscription that was updated.

currentSubscription.description: The type of your current Amazon Q Business subscription.

nextSubscription.description: The type of the Amazon Q Business subscription for the next month.

type.description: The type of an Amazon Q Business subscription.

userAliasesAdded.description: The user aliases that have been to be added to a user id.

userAliasesUpdated.description: The user aliases attached to a user id that have been updated.

userAliasesDeleted.description: The user aliases that have been deleted from a user id.

indexId.description: The identifier of the index that the user aliases are associated with.

dataSourceId.description: The identifier of the data source that the user aliases are associated with.

userId.description: The identifier of the user id associated with the user aliases.
